title: Dense Normandy Pecorino-Romano Rillettes
description: Peppery, juicy, and aromatic Normandy rillettes featuring Pecorino-Romano cheese, perfect for a casual dinner or snack. Serve with toasted bread or crackers for an effortless and flavorful meal.

ingredients.name: duck confit
ingredients.notes: canned or homemade, cooked to an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C)

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: used to cook the confit and facilitate blending

ingredients.name: dry white wine
ingredients.notes: used to add depth and moisture to the rillettes

ingredients.name: fresh thyme
ingredients.notes: used to add a subtle, herbaceous flavor

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: used to enrich the rillettes and add a creamy texture

instructions: In a blender or food processor, blend the Pecorino-Romano cheese, duck confit, water, and white wine until smooth and creamy.
Add the black pepper and thyme to the blender and blend until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a saucepan and add the unsalted butter. Heat over low heat, stirring constantly, until the butter has melted and the rillettes have reached a smooth, creamy consistency.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the rillettes cool to room temperature before serving.
Transfer the rillettes to a serving dish or ramekins and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow the flavors to meld and the cheese to firm up.
Serve the rillettes chilled, garnished with fresh thyme and crusty bread or crackers on the side.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
